column2: and each one has something special. Oh and I think I figured  out how I messed up the order of one of the pictures in the last few sets. I did it again only this time it was fixable since the last one required deleting and re-posting 7 pictures to fix

column1: 2004-07-17‚ÄéThe collection posted today is one of mixed subjects. I was too lazy to upload the pictures at the time so they collected on my computer and I had to upload them some day. They include pictures of my fencing club at Discover Days at the Shadbolt Center for the Arts
column2: as well as pictures at Lake Cheakamus
column3: a few in the city on the way home
column4: and one random one taken for no special reason. Well there is a reason. But it wasn't planned. It was amusing.

column1: 2004-07-17‚ÄéSam and Bob fencing. Damn their greatness!  urlLink

2004-07-17‚ÄéThis is Jimmy instructing a kid who was trying out fencing for the first time.  urlLink
2004-07-17‚ÄéJust the way I like the communists. On the ground and bird shitted on :) My family had so suffer through them in Romania
column2: I was too young but the stories are terrible. I was with my friend Kriselda when I took this picture. A man walked by and looked at me like I was crazy.  urlLink

2004-07-17‚ÄéAnother bear. This little (big) guy got himself caught in a bear trap right when we were getting in our car in the parking lot. They're relocating bears from the Whistler area because they're getting numerous (I saw 3 that day) and dangerous. I wish I could have gotten a better shot. You can't see the bear in this picture. I was afraid that it would go mad if I turned on the flash. Oh well... The memories...  urlLink

2004-07-17‚ÄéBlack bear! Well actually this one is brown. I nearly walked into it because I was getting ahead of the group hiking back to the car. It was so freaking close to me! I back tracked and then ran for it. When I came back with the rest of the group I took this picture. The bear was far away then.  urlLink
